data.title: U.S. API Distillate Stocks Decline by 599,000 Barrels
data.description: According to Jin10, the American Petroleum Institute (API) reported that U.S. distillate inventories decreased by 599,000 barrels for the week ending April 3. This figure was below the expected decline of 869,000 barrels and follows a previous decrease of 1.043 million barrels.

data.title: Iran Ready for Peace or Conflict, Says Senior Official
data.description: A senior Iranian official has stated that Iran is prepared for both peace and conflict. According to NS3.AI, this declaration underscores the country's readiness to navigate various geopolitical scenarios. The official emphasized Iran's strategic positioning and its ability to respond to different international developments. This statement comes amid ongoing tensions in the region, highlighting Iran's stance on maintaining its sovereignty and security.

data.title: U.S. API Gasoline Inventory Decline Exceeds Expectations
data.description: The American Petroleum Institute (API) reported a significant decrease in U.S. gasoline inventories for the week ending April 3. According to Jin10, the gasoline stockpiles fell by 3.968 million barrels, surpassing the anticipated decline of 1.409 million barrels. This reduction also exceeded the previous week's decrease of 3.209 million barrels. The larger-than-expected drop in gasoline inventories may impact market dynamics and fuel prices.

data.title: Pimco Considers $14 Billion Debt Financing for Oracle Data Center
data.description: Pacific Investment Management Company (Pimco) is reportedly contemplating a $14 billion debt financing deal for Oracle's data center, according to sources familiar with the matter. If finalized, Pimco would become a key supporter of Oracle's data center campus located in Saline, Michigan. Oracle plans to use the facility to provide services for OpenAI applications. Sources indicate that the financing might be structured as bonds, with Pimco potentially selling a portion of these bonds to other investors.

data.title: Sky Governance Proposes Upgrades to Enhance Sky Protocol's Stability
data.description: Sky Governance has put forward two structural upgrades aimed at bolstering the capital protection framework of Sky Protocol. According to NS3.AI, the proposal includes the introduction of a more robust solvency buffer and a sustainable staking rewards model. These measures are designed to enhance the long-term stability of the protocol.

data.title: Japan Expands Tax-Free Savings Accounts Amid Youth Investment Concerns
data.description: Japan has significantly increased the scope of its tax-free savings accounts, raising concerns about the investment behavior of its younger population. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that some experts worry that the youth are experiencing a fear of missing out (FOMO) as they seek higher returns. This expansion aims to encourage savings and investment among the Japanese population, but it also raises questions about the potential risks associated with inexperienced investors entering the market. The move is part of a broader strategy to stimulate economic growth and financial literacy in the country.

data.title: Survey Reveals Growing Interest in Crypto Payroll Among Employees
data.description:
A recent survey conducted by Oobit highlights a growing interest among full-time employees in receiving part of their paycheck in digital assets. According to NS3.AI, the survey, which included responses from 1,004 workers, found that 43% of participants expressed interest in a crypto payroll option. Despite this interest, only 7% reported that their employer currently offers such an option.

The study further revealed that 32% of employees would choose to receive part of their salary in cryptocurrency if their employer introduced the option immediately. However, concerns about the volatility of digital assets remain prevalent, with 88% of respondents indicating they are at least somewhat worried about fluctuations in value.

These findings suggest a potential shift in employee preferences towards digital assets, although the apprehension regarding market volatility continues to be a significant barrier to widespread adoption.

data.title: IMF Warns of Rising Global Public Debt Nearing World War II Levels
data.description: Global public debt is nearing 100% of the world's GDP, a level not seen since World War II, according to the International Monetary Fund (IMF). According to NS3.AI, an IMF chart published in F&D magazine highlights significant debt increases during major crises, with current projections indicating a continued rise rather than a decline following economic shocks. Era Dabla-Norris and Rodrigo Valdes emphasized the importance of trust as governments navigate the challenges of balancing spending, taxation, and debt servicing.

data.title: U.S. President Trump to Address Pakistan's Proposal on Iran
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump is set to respond to a proposal from Pakistan concerning Iran, according to White House spokesperson Levitt. The details of the proposal have not been disclosed, but the administration is expected to provide a response in the near future.

data.title: Suspects Charged in Crypto Wrench Attacks Using Fake Deliveries
data.description: Three individuals have been charged in connection with two separate crypto wrench attack incidents involving fake DoorDash and Uber Eats deliveries. According to NS3.AI, authorities suspect these men are part of a larger operation targeting significant cryptocurrency holders through similar criminal activities. The cases highlight the growing trend of using deceptive tactics to access digital assets unlawfully.

data.title: Morgan Stanley Analyst Predicts Prolonged Closure of Strait of Hormuz
data.description: Morgan Stanley energy analyst Martijn Rats has projected that the effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz, which has disrupted over 20% of the global oil flow, will persist until the end of April. According to NS3.AI, Rats anticipates that oil flows may only gradually recover following this period.

data.title: Iran Conflict Resolution Efforts Progress, Pakistani PM Urges Ceasefire
data.description: Pakistani Prime Minister has requested U.S. President Donald Trump to extend the deadline for Iran by two weeks, urging all parties involved in the conflict to cease hostilities for the same period to facilitate diplomatic efforts for a complete resolution of the war in Iran. According to Jin10, he emphasized that diplomatic efforts aimed at peacefully resolving the ongoing Middle Eastern conflict are steadily advancing. He also called on Iran to demonstrate goodwill by opening the Strait of Hormuz within the next two weeks. The market reacted swiftly to this information, with WTI crude oil dropping $2 in the short term, spot gold surging nearly $40 and breaking the $4,700 mark, while U.S. stocks saw a slight uptick.

data.title: AI TRENDS | MemPalace AI Tool Gains Traction with 10,000 GitHub Stars
data.description:
Milla Jovovich announced her involvement in developing MemPalace, an open-source AI knowledge tool that has quickly gained popularity. According to NS3.AI, the tool, which organizes documents in virtual rooms rather than using traditional keyword searches, achieved 10,000 GitHub stars and 50 pull requests within 24 hours of its release. Ben Sigman highlighted this rapid adoption as a significant milestone.

Sean Ren, a computer science professor at USC and CEO of Sahara AI, commented on the potential of MemPalace's method to be applied across various AI frameworks. However, he noted that the performance claims of the system have yet to be validated outside of controlled testing environments.

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | Spot Gold Surpasses $4,700 per Ounce with 1.08% Daily Increase
data.description: Spot gold prices have surged past $4,700 per ounce, marking a 1.08% increase within the day. According to Jin10, this rise in gold prices reflects ongoing market dynamics and investor sentiment. The precious metal continues to attract attention as a safe-haven asset amid global economic uncertainties. Analysts suggest that factors such as inflation concerns and geopolitical tensions may be contributing to the upward trend in gold prices. Investors are closely monitoring these developments as they assess the potential impact on their portfolios.

data.title: Gold Prices Surge as WTI Crude Dips $2 Amid Pakistan's Request to Trump
data.description: Gold prices experienced a short-term surge, while WTI crude oil saw a $2 drop. According to Jin10, Pakistan's Prime Minister has requested U.S. President Donald Trump to extend the deadline for Iran by two weeks. This development has influenced market dynamics, leading to fluctuations in commodity prices. The request from Pakistan comes amid ongoing geopolitical tensions, which continue to impact global markets.

data.title: JPMorgan CEO Warns of Inflation Risks Due to War-Related Supply Shocks
data.description: JPMorgan Chase CEO Jamie Dimon has cautioned shareholders about potential inflationary pressures in 2026, driven by supply shocks related to ongoing conflicts. According to NS3.AI, Dimon highlighted disruptions in energy, commodity products, shipbuilding, and farming as key factors emerging from the conflicts in Iran and Ukraine. He also referenced a Congressional Budget Office forecast predicting an increase in the US debt-to-GDP ratio from 100% currently to 120% by 2036.

data.title: U.S. February Consumer Credit Falls Short of Expectations
data.description: U.S. consumer credit for February was reported at $9.48 billion, according to Jin10. This figure fell short of the anticipated $10.25 billion. The previous value was revised from $8.05 billion to $7.67 billion.
